# /alm-extract

> Emit a `handoff.v1` (with payload validated against `alm-extract.v1`) consumed by the `alm` agent to push the feature's work-item hierarchy (L1 Epic / L2 Feature / L3 User Story / L4 Task) plus test plan + suites into ADO or JIRA.

## Usage

```
/alm-extract [--feature <slug>] [--project <name>]
```

## Inputs

- `projects/{p}/d365-ce/features/{f}/plan.md` (L1-L4 hierarchy)
- `projects/{p}/d365-ce/features/{f}/tasks/*.task.md` (L4 detail; `implementation-status` recorded)
- `projects/{p}/d365-ce/features/{f}/test-plan/**` (test cases for ADO Test Plans / JIRA Zephyr)
- `projects/{p}/d365-ce/features/{f}/spec.md` (FR-NN ids; AC text for ACs)
- `project.config.yaml almProvider: ado|jira`

## Execution flow

1. Resolve feature; assert `DOCUMENTED` is in `currentStates` (otherwise warn that handoff will reflect mid-build state).
2. Build the L1-L4 tree from plan.md.
3. Attach AC text from spec FRs to L3 User Stories.
4. Attach test cases to L4 tasks via `test-coverage` field.
5. Build `alm-extract.v1` payload (per [schemas/alm-extract.v1.json](../../schemas/alm-extract.v1.json)).
6. Wrap in `handoff.v1` envelope:
   - `sourceAgent: d365-ce`, `sourceFeature: {slug}`
   - `targetAgent: alm`, `targetArtifact: alm-extract`
   - `payload: { ...alm-extract.v1 contents }`
7. Validate against `schemas/handoff.v1.json` and `schemas/alm-extract.v1.json`.
8. Write to `projects/{p}/_handoffs/d365-ce-to-alm-{feature}.handoff.json`.
9. Update `.workflow.json` history (no gate transition; handoff is a side-effect).

## Output

- `projects/{p}/_handoffs/d365-ce-to-alm-{feature}.handoff.json`

## ID strategy

- L1 Epic uid: `ce-{feature-slug}-L1-01`
- L2 Feature uid: `ce-{feature-slug}-L2-NN` per sub-platform
- L3 User Story uid: `ce-{feature-slug}-L3-NN` per spec FR-NN
- L4 Task uid: `ce-{feature-slug}-L4-NN` per task card
